hints.technical: You are improving "{{p1}}", but "{{p2}}" is getting worse. Inventors solved this as follows:
hints.physical: Physical contradiction: the parameter "{{p1}}" must meet opposing requirements. TRIZ suggests separation in time or space.
hints.empty: No ready-made recipes for this pair yet. Try more general parameters.

1.name: Weight of moving object
1.desc: Mass of a body or system in motion.

2.name: Weight of non-moving object
2.desc: Mass of an object at rest.

3.name: Length of moving object
3.desc: Any linear dimension (length, width, height) of an object in motion.

4.name: Length of non-moving object
4.desc: Linear dimensions of a stationary object.

5.name: Area of moving object
5.desc: Size of the outer or inner surface of a body in motion.

6.name: Area of non-moving object
6.desc: Surface area of a stationary object.

7.name: Volume of moving object
7.desc: Space occupied by an object during motion.

8.name: Volume of non-moving object
8.desc: Space occupied by a stationary object.

9.name: Speed
9.desc: Rate of object movement or process pace.

10.name: Force
10.desc: Interaction that changes the state of an object (pressure, load).

11.name: Stress or pressure
11.desc: Force per unit area or electrical voltage.

12.name: Shape
12.desc: External appearance or contours of an object.

13.name: Stability of object composition
13.desc: Integrity of the system and its resistance to breakdown.

14.name: Strength
14.desc: Ability to withstand loads without breaking.

15.name: Duration of action of moving object
15.desc: Period during which an object can perform work.

16.name: Duration of action of non-moving object
16.desc: Durability and service life of a stationary device.

17.name: Temperature
17.desc: Thermal state of the object or environment.

19.name: Energy consumption (moving object)
19.desc: Amount of energy or fuel required for operation.

21.name: Power
21.desc: Rate of doing work or energy transfer.

22.name: Loss of Energy
22.desc: Waste of resources not used for useful work.

23.name: Loss of Substance
23.desc: Leaks, waste of materials or raw materials.

24.name: Loss of Information
24.desc: Errors in data transmission or loss of knowledge.

26.name: Quantity of substance
26.desc: Consumption of materials, number of components in the system.

28.name: Measurement accuracy
28.desc: How close a measurement is to reality.

29.name: Manufacturing precision
29.desc: Compliance of the finished product with specified drawings.

30.name: Object-affected harmful factors
30.desc: Environmental influence (corrosion, noise, vibration) on the system.

31.name: Object-generated harmful factors
31.desc: Harm caused by the object itself (toxic emissions, noise).

32.name: Manufacturability
32.desc: How easy and cheap it is to produce the object.

33.name: User-friendliness
33.desc: Simplicity and comfort for human use.

34.name: Maintainability
34.desc: Speed and ease of returning to a working state after failure.

35.name: Adaptability
35.desc: Ability to adjust to different operating modes.

36.name: Device complexity
36.desc: Number of parts and intricacy of internal connections.

37.name: Control complexity
37.desc: How difficult it is to control the process.

38.name: Level of automation
38.desc: The share of work performed without human intervention.

39.name: Productivity
39.desc: Amount of useful output per unit of time.
